title: caltha thracica
intro: Caltha thracica, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that is native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Caltha thracica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. However, it's essential to note that Caltha thracica can be toxic in large quantities, and its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional.
